mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during post-shock recovery periods, with forward returns positive in >60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive policy creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands consumer spending capacity and supports corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >65% of periods with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears that force rapid portfolio deleveraging, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a supply-demand rebalancing that boosts equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances during early-cycle recovery, or the joint signal loses significance (p>0.10) in multivariate models.
